name: Voyage and Return
description: A protagonist enters an unfamiliar world, survives its rules and dangers, and returns home with insight or maturity.

nodes.label: Familiar World
nodes.definition: The protagonist's known environment, which feels limited, stifling, or simply normal. Something about the character is restless or incomplete.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the character's dissatisfaction or innocence
exit_conditions: A passage to the unfamiliar world opens
typical_variants: Bored child in a mundane setting
Character at the edge of a boundary they've been told not to cross
failure_modes: Familiar world is too pleasant — no reason to leave
Character's restlessness is stated but not shown

nodes.label: Passage Into the Unknown
nodes.definition: The protagonist crosses into a radically different world — by accident, curiosity, or force. The crossing is sudden and disorienting.
entry_conditions: A portal, accident, or transgression occurs
exit_conditions: The protagonist is fully in the unfamiliar world with no immediate way back
typical_variants: Shipwreck or crash in alien territory
failure_modes: Crossing is too gradual to feel like a shock
The character can trivially return at any time

nodes.label: Fascination Phase
nodes.definition: The protagonist explores the new world with wonder, excitement, or naive confidence. The world seems marvelous and the character feels liberated.
entry_conditions: Character is in the unfamiliar world
Initial danger is not yet apparent
exit_conditions: The world's darker rules begin to surface
failure_modes: Fascination phase is so long the story stalls

nodes.label: Shadow Emerges
nodes.definition: The unfamiliar world reveals its dangerous, oppressive, or incomprehensible aspects. What seemed wondrous now threatens the protagonist.
entry_conditions: A darker element is triggered or discovered
exit_conditions: The protagonist recognizes that the world is hostile and they are vulnerable
typical_variants: Natural law of the world threatens survival
The world's beauty masks a trap
failure_modes: Shadow appears abruptly with no buildup
Shadow is too mild to create genuine fear
signals_in_text: Realization that the rules are different

nodes.label: Frustration and Imprisonment
nodes.definition: The protagonist is trapped, pursued, or constrained by the unfamiliar world's power. The initial delight is replaced by desperation to survive or escape.
exit_conditions: The protagonist finds or creates a means of escape
typical_variants: Pursuit by the world's ruling force
Psychological entrapment (loss of identity or memory)
failure_modes: Imprisonment feels arbitrary rather than consequential
Protagonist is passive and waits for rescue

nodes.label: Thrilling Escape
nodes.definition: The protagonist breaks free from the unfamiliar world's grip in a climactic sequence. Success depends on something learned during the voyage.
entry_conditions: A means of escape is identified
The stakes are at their peak
exit_conditions: The protagonist is on the path back to the familiar world
typical_variants: Solving a riddle or breaking a spell
Sacrifice that opens the way home
failure_modes: Escape is too easy — no climax
Escape depends on luck rather than growth
Escape is achieved by someone else

nodes.label: Return Changed
nodes.definition: The protagonist returns to the familiar world carrying insight, maturity, or a new perspective. The familiar world is the same but the character sees it differently.
entry_conditions: The character is back in the familiar world
exit_conditions: The character demonstrates that the voyage has changed them
typical_variants: Return treated as waking from a dream
Return with proof of the voyage (an object, scar, or story)
Return to find the familiar world now feels small
failure_modes: Character seems unchanged by the experience
Return is rushed with no reflection
The voyage's lessons are never applied
signals_in_text: New appreciation or dissatisfaction with home

edges.label: Crosses Over
edges.preconditions: Familiar world is established; a portal or accident occurs
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character is separated from all known support; survival is now the baseline concern
edges.effects_on_character: Forced out of complacency into radical novelty
edges.common_alternatives: Character is pulled into the other world rather than stumbling in
edges.anti_patterns: Crossing is so gentle there's no sense of dislocation

edges.label: Begins Exploring
edges.preconditions: Character is in the unfamiliar world; immediate danger is not yet present
edges.effects_on_stakes: Low immediate danger but the character is investing in a world they don't understand
edges.common_alternatives: Fascination begins immediately upon arrival; fascination develops slowly
edges.anti_patterns: Character is never fascinated — the world is hostile from the start (this is a different archetype)

edges.label: Darkness Appears
edges.preconditions: Fascination has built false confidence; a darker reality surfaces
edges.effects_on_stakes: The world is now genuinely dangerous; the character cannot simply enjoy it
edges.effects_on_character: Innocence or naivety is punctured; fear and caution emerge
edges.common_alternatives: Shadow was always visible but the character ignored it
edges.anti_patterns: Transition to danger is arbitrary with no seeds planted during fascination

edges.label: Becomes Trapped
edges.preconditions: The shadow is active; the character tries and fails to avoid it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Survival is now the primary concern; returning home seems impossible
edges.effects_on_character: Desperation replaces curiosity; the character's true resilience is tested
edges.common_alternatives: Trapping is immediate and violent; trapping is gradual as the world closes in
edges.anti_patterns: Character is never truly trapped; escape is always obviously available

edges.label: Breaks Free
edges.preconditions: A means of escape is discovered or created; the character commits to the attempt
edges.effects_on_stakes: All-or-nothing moment; failure means permanent loss
edges.effects_on_character: Character applies everything learned; agency is reclaimed
edges.common_alternatives: Escape is aided by an ally from the fascination phase
edges.anti_patterns: Escape is handed to the character; no earned capability is required

edges.label: Returns Home
edges.preconditions: Escape is successful; the path back to the familiar world is open
edges.effects_on_stakes: External danger dissolves; the internal stakes now dominate (has the character changed?)
edges.effects_on_character: Character must integrate the voyage's lessons into ordinary life
edges.common_alternatives: Return is ambiguous (was it real?); return involves proving the voyage happened
edges.anti_patterns: Return is instantaneous with no transition; character shows no change
